prose: The positive call rests on curated CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence [E1], a diagnostic-grade mechanistic anchor that fixes this recovery at the curated_anchored_only tier. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis is coherent: the molecular initiating event is grounded in MitoCarta3.0 Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the adverse outcome localizing to the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2], consistent with the attached AOP pathway. Assay data are only corroborative and here are notably thin, reflecting 6-hydroxydopamine's status as a research reagent [E3]; their sparsity is anti-diagnostic and does not weaken the curated anchor. Structure-based prediction flags the compound as non-brain-penetrant [E4], but this is expected—it is a classical lesioning agent delivered directly or entering via transporters, so predicted passive impermeability does not undercut the mechanistic plausibility. Overall, the curated mechanism alone justifies the positive call, with mitochondrial and cell-type grounding strengthening biological plausibility rather than replacing the anchor.

citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP 3: Inhibition of the mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (WPHA/WNT Endorsed).

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
citations.reference: Rath S, Sharma R, Gupta R, et al. MitoCarta3.0: an updated mitochondrial proteome now with sub-organelle localization and pathway annotations. Nucleic Acids Research. 2021;49(D1):D1541-D1547. Kamath T, Abdulraouf A, Burris SJ, et al. Single-cell genomic profiling of human dopamine neurons identifies a population that selectively degenerates in Parkinson's disease. Nature Neuroscience. 2022;25(5):588-595.
U.S. EPA ToxCast/Tox21 invitroDB (v4.2), accessed through the NICEATM Integrated Chemical Environment (ICE). Active hitcalls only; queried on the salt-form-correct record.

citations.detail: Non-penetrant by structure (e.g. lesioning agent delivered directly / enters via transporters).
citations.reference: Meng F, Xi Y, Huang J, Ayers PW. A curated diverse molecular database of blood-brain barrier permeability with chemical descriptors (B3DB). Scientific Data. 2021;8:289. Daina A, Zoete V. A BOILED-Egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain access of small molecules. ChemMedChem. 2016;11(11):1117-1121.
